common.selectImageFile: Please select an image file (PNG, JPG, etc.).
common.mustBeLoggedIn: You must be logged in to upload images.
common.imageLoadFailed: Could not load this image (wrong address, blocked, or offline).

home.trialPricingLine: 30-day free trial, then €x/month. The exact amount x is always confirmed before you subscribe.
home.title: Study more effectively
home.subtitle: Build exercises for languages, vocabulary, or any subject. Practice with straight answer, flashcards, or multiple choice; answer by typing or by voice; take timed exams — all in your browser.
home.feature1: Straight answer, flashcards, and multiple choice
home.feature2: Flashcards with listen mode and speech-friendly gaps
home.feature3: Speech recognition: speak answers in practice (Chrome / Edge; optional cloud STT when enabled)
home.feature7: Works on any device in the browser
home.feature8: No installation — always up to date

login.checkEmailText: We sent a password reset link to {email}. Click the link in the email to set a new password.
login.passwordUpdated: Password updated. You can now log in.
login.confirmEmail: Check your email to confirm your account, then log in.
login.authLinkInvalid: The reset link could not be used (it may have expired, or your email app opened it without the full address). Request a new link with “Forgot password?” below, or open the link in Safari, Chrome, or Edge instead of the in-app email browser.

dashboard.duplicateDeckSuccess: Copy created — you can edit the new deck.
dashboard.noDecksHint: Create a new deck or import an existing PSTUDY .txt file.
dashboard.mergeDecksHint: Select two or more decks, then merge them into one new deck. Items are appended in selection order. Your original decks stay as they are.
dashboard.mergeDecksOrderHint: Items will appear in deck order: first selected deck, then the next, and so on.
dashboard.mergeDecksKeepOriginals: Original decks are not deleted or changed.
dashboard.mergeDecksNeedTwo: Select at least two decks to merge.
dashboard.mergeDecksSelectPrompt: Tick the decks to combine, in the order you want their items to appear in the new deck.
dashboard.filterDecksHint: Filter by title, field, topic, and deck language — the same options as in PstudyCommunity.
dashboard.noDecksMatchFilters: No decks match these filters. Try different criteria or clear the filters.

account.subtitle: A snapshot of your PSTUDY activity.
account.decksSharedHint: Draft and checked apply only to decks you share with PstudyCommunity.
account.examsIssuedHint: Timed exams you created and sent to others (Exams in the header).
account.examsToTakeHint: Invitations still open for you to take (My assigned exams).
account.communitySharedHint: Decks with “Share with PstudyCommunity” enabled in the editor.
account.communitiesHint: Organizations you belong to for MyCommunities (shared decks inside your school, company, or team).
account.communitiesEmpty: You are not a member of any organization yet. If your school or team uses MyCommunities, an administrator can add you or send an invitation to your email.
account.aiCreditsDefault: This site does not show a personal AI credit balance. Generation uses the server’s configured API key—check with your administrator if your organisation tracks usage separately.

deck.checkedCannotSave: This deck is checked and can no longer be edited. Duplicate it to make changes.
deck.checkedReadOnlyBody: Peer reviewers have marked this deck as checked. It cannot be changed. Duplicate the deck to edit your copy, then you can delete this original from My decks if you no longer need it.
deck.checkedCommunitySectionHint: Sharing settings and classification are frozen for checked decks.
deck.checkedFieldReadOnlyHint: This field cannot be edited (checked deck).
deck.sharingOptionsHint: Set organization visibility (Not shared, teachers only, or MyCommunities) and whether the deck is listed in the public PstudyCommunity directory. These are independent.
deck.myCommunitiesRequiresOrgHint: Join an organization in MyCommunities to unlock the organization options to the left of PstudyCommunity.
deck.shareFillClassification: Select field, topic, and one or two deck languages to publish on PstudyCommunity.
deck.shareIncompleteHint: Choose field, topic, and at least one deck language so others can find your deck.
deck.fillThisInstructionForAll: Fill this instruction for all items
deck.keywordsHint: Optional phrases (comma or semicolon): highlight on card backs after you flip, and gaps in Keyword cloze practice wherever they appear. Tag text in Description or Answer — e.g. for “Ask for description” exercises, mark words in the target-language Description so cloze drills that side; tags also match inside the Answer when relevant.
deck.contentLanguageHint: Your language for this deck (independent of field and topic), using the same list as practice → Speech language. PstudyCommunity search can filter by it.
deck.classificationFreeTextHint: Use the list first. If you do not see your field or topic, pick the “type your own” option at the bottom, enter a short name, and leave the box — it is checked for safety. Your new labels are saved in this browser and appear in the list next time.
deck.classificationTypeOwnField: Not in the list — type your own
deck.classificationTypeOwnTopic: Not in the list — type your own
deck.classificationApplyHint: Click outside this box or press Enter to save your text.
deck.classificationSessionRequired: Your session expired. Sign in again, then re-enter the field and topic.
deck.classificationModerationSkippedSave: The online safety check was unavailable, but your text was saved.
deck.classificationTooLong: Field and topic can be at most {max} characters each.
deck.classificationModerationFailed: That text did not pass the automatic safety check. Try different wording, or use an option from the list.
deck.classificationModerationUnavailable: The safety check could not run. Try again in a moment, or pick from the suggested field and topic lists.
deck.classificationUserListHint: You can remove labels you added here if no deck in your account still uses them. Built-in list entries cannot be removed.
deck.classificationRemoveInUseField: That field is still set on at least one of your decks. Change or clear it on those decks before removing it from this list.
deck.classificationRemoveInUseTopic: That field and topic pair is still set on at least one of your decks. Change or clear it before removing it from this list.
deck.keywordMarkerHint: Available when editing Description or Answer. Add: saves one phrase — your selection is grown to full words at both ends. Remove: deletes any saved keyword that overlaps your selection. You can also edit the Keywords column directly (commas or semicolons between phrases).
deck.dictationHint: Inserts at the cursor (Chrome / Edge). Language follows Practice → Speech language when set in this browser.
deck.columnFiltersHint: Show or hide columns in the table (saved in this browser for this deck).

practice.flashcardSttGoogle: Google Cloud (open transcription — no deck word forcing)
practice.flashcardSttGoogleUnavailable: Google is not configured on this server — the browser is used until you set credentials.
practice.flashcardFormatHint: Type or dictate the hidden side below. Speak mode writes what the recognizer heard — it does not force text to match the official answer; you compare after flipping. Optional keyword tags (deck editor) highlight only on the flipped backs: green when the word appears in both your answer and the official text, red on the official side when you omitted that keyword. Use new lines and bullet lines (- , * , • ) for structure.
practice.flashcardBrowseHint: Flip through the deck without typing an answer. Turn this off when you want to practice for real.
practice.flashcardBrowseKeys: Enter: show the back, then move to the next card. Use the buttons anytime.
practice.flashcardAllKnownMessage: Every card in this deck is marked as known, so there is nothing left in “unknown only” mode. Switch to all cards, or tap “Still learning (unmark)” on cards you want to review again.
practice.flashcardTypeBack: Your answer — other side of the card
practice.flashcardYourBackPlaceholder: Type the answer. New line = new line; for bullets start with "- ", "* ", or "• ".
practice.flashcardEnterToFlip: Enter starts a new line; Shift+Enter flips the card. Bullets use the same markers as in the deck editor.
practice.sttHeardRawLabel: Heard (top STT line before deck filter)
practice.yourAnswer: Your answer (Enter, Go, or Check below)
practice.mcSelectThenConfirm: Select an option, then tap Check answer or press Enter to submit.
practice.speechInputUnavailable: Speech input is not available: use Chrome or Edge, or enable Google Speech and turn on “Consider only deck answers” (straight mode) or use Flashcard mode.
practice.speechMcNoMic: Speech (Speak) is only available in straight answer or flashcard mode — not in multiple choice.
practice.deckSttAliasesPausesExercise: Practice listening is paused while this panel is open; close it to resume.
practice.deckSttAliasesHint: When the recognizer keeps returning the wrong word (e.g. music syllables), map what it hears to a deck answer. Stored only in this browser for this deck. Works with “Consider only deck answers” (Google STT) or with standard browser speech when that option is off—aliases are applied to your answer without requiring deck-only mode.
practice.keywordClozeMode: Keyword cloze — show the answer but hide keyword tags
practice.keywordClozeHint: Uses keyword tags from the deck editor. You see the full expected answer except those tagged words (gaps). The answer box starts with ___ where each keyword belongs; speak or type the full line — when speech is recognized as complete, the box fills with the official answer. “Consider only deck answers” is bypassed here so full sentences are not reduced to keywords only. Bullets (- * •) and line breaks match the card. In flashcard mode, “Reveal answer” only works after your text matches the full back.
practice.keywordClozeMustMatch: Your answer does not match the full card yet, including every keyword.
practice.keywordClozeNoKeywordsHint: This card has no keyword tags (min. 2 characters per tag). Add tags in the deck editor, or turn off keyword cloze.

import.title: Import PSTUDY .txt file
import.dropHint: Drop a .txt file here or click to browse
import.pasteNote: You can also click the box and paste (Ctrl+V).
import.dropZoneCompact: Drop a .txt file here, click to browse, or paste (Ctrl+V) when the area is selected.
import.dropTxtOnly: Please drop a .txt file or a plain-text file.
import.fileMustBeTxt: Please choose a .txt file (or plain text).
import.fileReadError: Could not read the file. Try exporting again or copy-paste the contents into the box.
import.deckMetaUpdateFailed: The deck was imported, but field, topic, or language could not be saved. You can set them in the deck editor.
import.imageImportToStorageFailed: {count} image(s) could not be copied to your library (the site may block automated downloads). The deck was imported — you can add pictures in the card editor.
import.aiSectionIntro: Paste or upload plain text (notes, article, book excerpt). PSTUDY asks an AI model for roughly the target number of flashcards or MC items — not an exhaustive index of an entire long file. Only the first portion of the text is sent (see the character limit below); for multi‑chapter books or 50+ page manuals, split the source into sections and run Generate several times (one deck per section works well). If you choose both flashcards and MC, PSTUDY saves two separate decks. Your text goes to the AI provider on the server — do not use for confidential material unless your policy allows it.
import.aiOpenTextEditor: Or type / paste in the text box
import.aiDeckLanguageLabel: Language of generated cards and questions
import.aiMcWrongOptionsLegend: Incorrect options per question (the correct answer is separate)
import.aiGenerating: Generating… this can take up to a minute.
import.aiSuccessTwoDecks: Two decks created — flashcards and multiple choice. Open them from My decks.
import.aiBothCreatesTwoDecksNote: You get two separate decks so flashcards and multiple choice can each be practiced in the right mode.
import.aiErrorGeneric: Generation failed. Try again or shorten the text.
import.aiErrorNotConfigured: AI is not configured on this server (OPENAI_API_KEY). Ask your administrator.
import.aiTruncatedNote: Note: only the beginning of your text was sent (server character limit). Split long files into parts and generate again for the rest.
import.aiExtraItemsDroppedNote: Note: the model returned many more items than your target (over 20% extra). PSTUDY dropped {count} row(s) from the saved deck; kept rows are sampled evenly across the full list. Two PSTUDY .txt files should download: the full AI output and the capped version—import either here if you want to change what you keep.
import.aiTxtExportsDownloaded: Two PSTUDY .txt files downloaded: full AI output (-ai-full) and PSTUDY-capped deck (-ai-pstudy-capped).
import.aiDocumentOverLimit: This text is {usedChars} characters; only the first {maxChars} are used. Split the file into smaller parts and run Generate on each part to cover a long document.
import.aiDocumentNearLimit: {usedChars} / {maxChars} characters — you are close to the server limit; anything beyond the first {maxChars} is ignored.
import.aiDocumentCharBudget: {usedChars} / {maxChars} characters (only text up to this limit is sent to the AI).

community.browseHint: Browse decks shared by others. Copy any deck to your collection to edit and practice. Filter by field, topic, or deck language like in the row below.
community.shareHint: Share your own decks from the deck editor by turning on “Share with PstudyCommunity”. Set “Deck language” there so others can find your deck.
community.title: PstudyCommunity decks
community.deckLanguageFilterHint: Open to choose one or more languages. Optionally include decks with no language set.
community.includeNoLanguage: Include decks with no language set

school.title: MyCommunities library
school.browseHint: Decks shared within your organization (company, school, or team). MyCommunities items are visible to students only after a teacher or admin verifies them.
school.noMembership: You are not a member of an organization with MyCommunities access yet.
school.visibilityNoneHint: This deck is not listed in the selected organization’s MyCommunities library.
school.alreadyVerified: This deck is already verified for this organization.
school.sectionHint: Share with your organization separately from PstudyCommunity. Students only see MyCommunities decks after teacher verification.
school.notShared: Not shared with the selected organization
school.saveShareNothingToUpdate: Already not shared for this organization. Change the option above, then save.
school.shareRemoved: This deck is no longer shared with that organization.
school.studentsNeedVerification: Students in your organization will only see this after a teacher or admin verifies it.
school.ownerReadOnly: MyCommunities sharing is managed by the deck owner.
school.roleRequiredTeachersOnly: Only teachers and admins can use “Teachers only” visibility.

orgAdmin.intro: Add teachers and students to your organization, change their roles, or send email invitations. People must use the email address you enter (or receive the invite at) when they sign in to PSTUDY.
orgAdmin.addPeopleHint: If they already have a PSTUDY account with this email, use “Add existing account”. Otherwise use “Send email invitation” (needs email configured on the server).
orgAdmin.inviteCreatedNoEmail: Invitation created, but email could not be sent (configure RESEND_API_KEY). Share the invite link manually if needed.
orgAdmin.removeConfirm: Remove this person from the organization? They lose access to MyCommunities-shared content.
orgAdmin.loadOrgsFailed: Could not load organizations you administer.
orgAdmin.notAdmin: You are not an organization admin in PSTUDY yet. The first admin is set in the database; ask your administrator.
orgAdmin.inviteBody: You have been invited to join “{school}” as a {role}. This invite was sent to an address like {email}.
orgAdmin.inviteSignInFirst: Sign in (or create an account) using the same email address the invitation was sent to.
orgAdmin.inviteInvalid: This invitation is invalid or expired.
orgAdmin.acceptAlreadyMember: You were already a member; invitation closed.

exam.title: Exams
exam.subtitle: Create timed multiple-choice exams from your decks and invite people by email.
exam.promptDescription: Show the explanation; pick the correct description
exam.promptExplanation: Show the description; pick the correct explanation
exam.emailsHint: One per line, or separate with commas
exam.validationOk: This deck is OK to use as a multiple-choice exam.
exam.validationFail: Fix the issues below before you can create the exam.
exam.needTwoItems: Add at least two items to this deck.
exam.inviteHelp: Each examinee has their own link. Copy it and send it any way you like. Use “More…” only if you need a fresh link or to cancel an invite.
exam.reissueDisabledInProgress: Wait until this exam is not in progress before creating a new link.
exam.revokeConfirm: Cancel this invite? This person will not be able to use their link anymore.
exam.noExamsYet: You have not created any exams yet.
exam.schemaMissing: Database tables for exams are not installed yet. In Supabase → SQL Editor, run the script: supabase/exam-schema.sql
exam.takePlaceholder: Timed exam mode for this link will run here. You can close this page for now.
exam.myAssignedExamsHint: Open your exam links to start or resume. Results appear after submission.
exam.noAssignedExams: No exams assigned to your account yet.
exam.deleteAssignedExamTitle: Delete this exam from your list?
exam.deleteAssignedExamMessage: Your invite link will stop working and this exam will disappear from My assigned exams. The organiser will see the invitation as cancelled. To take the exam again, they must send a new invite.
exam.deleteExamMessage: This removes the exam assignment, all invitation links, and stored results. Your deck is not deleted. This cannot be undone.

examValidation.emptyCorrect: Missing correct answer text for this item.
examValidation.emptyPrompt: Missing question text (opposite field or instruction).
examValidation.notEnoughChoices: Need at least two different answer options (correct + distractors).
examValidation.emptyExpectedForStraight: Missing expected answer for straight-answer grading.

help.intro: PSTUDY Online lets you build study decks, practice in different modes (including voice), share decks on PstudyCommunity, and run timed exams. This page is a practical guide: how things work, and—in particular—how to import existing word lists or exam material from `.txt` or spreadsheet exports without retyping, and how **AI generation** on the import page can turn notes into a starter deck.
help.bodyGettingStarted:
Create an account or log in. Your **My decks** page lists every deck you own. Use **New deck** to start empty, or **Import .txt** to load a PSTUDY text file. Open a deck to edit items (question and answer text, optional instruction line, picture, and multiple-choice distractors for exams).

**Account** summarizes your decks, exams, and other activity.

Use the header links to open **PstudyCommunity** (public decks from others), **MyCommunities** (shared decks inside your organization, if you belong to one—see **MyCommunities (organizations)**), **My assigned exams** (invitations sent to your email), **Import .txt**, and **Exams** (exams you created).
help.bodyDecks:
Each deck has **items**. The **description** and **explanation** fields are the main question and answer sides; which one is shown as the prompt in practice depends on **Ask for** (description vs explanation) on the practice screen.

You can set **Field** and **Topic** for organisation and PstudyCommunity browsing. **Share with PstudyCommunity** makes a deck discoverable on the PstudyCommunity page (others copy it into their own collection; your original stays yours).

When a deck is **shared**, the editor header shows **Draft** or **Checked** (PstudyCommunity quality). While it is **Draft**, use **Peer review** in the toolbar (next to **Create exam** and **Practice**) to invite a reviewer by email and send the invitation from the dialog. For what **Draft** and **Checked** mean, and what reviewers can do, see **PstudyCommunity quality and peer review** below.

**Create exam** and **Practice** appear above the item table. Pictures upload to storage and autosave with the rest of the deck.

To combine decks you already own into one new deck, see **Merging decks** below.
help.bodyMergeDecks:
On **My decks**, you can copy several decks into a **single new deck** without losing the originals.

**Requirements.** You must have **at least two** decks. The **Merge decks** button sits to the **left** of **New deck**; it stays disabled until you have two or more decks.

**Steps.** Press **Merge decks** to turn on merge mode—the button is highlighted, and a **checkbox** appears next to each deck. Tick every deck you want to include. **The order you tick them** is the order their items will appear in the new deck (all items from the first ticked deck, then all from the second, and so on). Use **Clear selection** to untick everything without leaving merge mode. Press **Continue**, enter a **title** for the merged deck, and confirm. When finished, PSTUDY opens the **new** deck in the editor.

**What changes.** PSTUDY **creates one new deck** with a **fresh copy** of every item (new internal IDs). Your **original decks are not deleted, renamed, or edited**. The merged deck starts as **private** (not shared). **Field** and **Topic** are copied from the **first** deck you ticked—you can change them in the editor. Click **Merge decks** again, or **Done merging**, to leave merge mode and hide the checkboxes.

Merging is useful after **Import .txt**, **AI generation**, or splitting work across chapters and you want one combined deck for practice or exams.
help.bodyPractice:
From a deck, open **Practice**. Choose **Straight answer**, **Multiple choice**, or **Flashcard**.

**Straight answer** and **multiple choice** use the same **Ask for** setting (description vs explanation) so you control which side of the card is the question.

**Order** can be normal or random. **Listen** reads the question aloud when that mode is on (instruction plus main line when both exist). **Speak** uses the microphone where your browser allows; on straight answer, optional cloud speech (when the server is configured) can favour answers that appear in the deck.

**Exercise setup** can be collapsed to focus on the question. After you submit, the result panel shows correct/incorrect; press **Next** (or Enter) to continue. **Repeat mistakes** appears when you finish a round with wrong answers so you can drill only those items.

While results are shown, the question image and text stay on the item you just answered so nothing jumps ahead early.

**Flashcard** practice is covered in the next section—flip cards, optional typing or dictation, keywords, and known/unknown filtering.
help.bodyFlashcards:
In **Practice**, choose **Flashcard** to study with a two-sided card that **flips** in 3D. The **front** is the side you are asked first; the **back** is the official answer. Which field is treated as the “question” still follows **Ask for** (description vs explanation), like straight answer—so you can show either side first depending on how you built the deck.

**Before you flip**, type your version of the answer in the box, or turn on **Speak** and dictate it. In the text box, **Enter** starts a new line; **Shift+Enter** flips the card to reveal the back. After the back is shown, **Enter** often moves you on—watch the short hint under the controls for the exact behaviour. With **Speak** on, you can choose **Browser** listening or **Google** speech when your host supports cloud recognition.

**Listen** (when enabled) reads the question side—instruction and main line together when both are filled in.

Optional **keywords** in the deck editor (comma- or semicolon-separated) mark important phrases. After you flip, keywords are **highlighted** on the official answer and on what you typed: you can see at a glance what you included or skipped.

The back can use **several lines** of text. Lines beginning with `-`, `*`, or `•` are shown as a **bullet list** after you reveal.

**Which cards appear:** you can practise **All cards** or only those you have **not** marked as known (**Unknown only**). The count of **known** cards is shown in the strip of options. “Known” is saved **in this browser only** (not in your account), so another device or browser starts fresh unless you mark cards there too.

**Browse only** is for a lighter pass: no typing or dictation—flip with the buttons, then mark **Known** or **Not known** for each card.

Speech works best in **Chrome** or **Edge**; other browsers may limit **Listen** or **Speak**.
help.bodyImport:
Use **Import .txt** in the header (log in first). You can upload a `.txt` file, drop it on the import area, or paste from the clipboard. PSTUDY always creates a **new deck** containing the imported items—open it after import to rename the deck and fix anything that looks off.

**What this is for.** The format is the same as the classic **PSTUDY desktop** `.txt` export, so you can move old decks into PSTUDY Online. You can also prepare data in **Excel**, **Google Sheets**, or another spreadsheet (**one row per card**) and export or copy as **plain text with Tab characters between columns**, then save as UTF-8 `.txt`. That avoids retyping long word lists by hand.

**One non-empty line = one item.** Completely blank lines are ignored.

**Fields are separated with the Tab key, not commas.** Each line is split on Tab characters only. Commas inside your text stay part of the field. A standard comma-separated CSV from Excel will **not** import correctly until you convert it to **tab-delimited** text (for example Text (Tab delimited) in Excel, or a sheet that uses tabs when exporting).

**Column order left to right (always the same):** 1) **Description** — question / prompt side. 2) **Explanation** — answer side. 3–6) **Multiple choice 1–4** — wrong-answer options used in multiple choice and exams (may be left empty). 7) **Picture** — optional image URL (`https://…`), or empty. Older desktop `.txt` files may contain a long Base64 block instead; the importer tries to recognise it. 8) **Instruction** — optional extra line shown with the prompt (for example: translate into which language). 9) **Keywords** — optional tags for **flashcard** mode (comma- or semicolon-separated); may be left empty.

If a line has fewer than nine Tab-separated values, missing trailing fields count as empty. Lines where **both** description and explanation are empty are skipped.

You can leave all four multiple-choice fields empty if you only use straight answer or flashcards, but you must still use **Tabs** so that picture, instruction, and keywords stay in the correct columns when you need them.

Prefer **UTF-8** when saving the file. After import, review items in the deck editor—especially punctuation, pictures, and special letters.
help.bodyAiGenerate:
On the same page as **Import .txt**, the section **Generate deck from text (AI)** builds a **new deck** (or two decks) from plain text you paste or upload. You must be **logged in**. The server sends your text to an AI model (OpenAI). If **AI is not configured** on the host (`OPENAI_API_KEY`), generation will not run—ask whoever runs the site.

**What to expect.** You choose **flashcards only**, **multiple choice only**, or **both**, plus target counts per mode (within the allowed range shown on the form), how many wrong options each MC question has, and optionally the **language** of the generated cards and a **deck title**. If you pick **both**, PSTUDY saves **two** decks—one for flashcards and one for multiple choice—so each can be practiced in the right mode.

Generation aims at roughly your **target** number of items, not a full index of a very long book or manual. Only the **first portion** of the text is sent (currently up to about **40,000 characters**); the import page shows how much of your text counts toward that limit. For long sources, split the text into sections and run **Generate** several times (one deck per section often works well).

**Privacy.** Your source text is processed on the server and by the AI provider—do **not** use for confidential material unless your policy allows it.

**When the model returns more rows than your targets.** If the total number of items is **at most 20% above** your configured target (flashcard target, MC target, or **both added together** when you asked for both), PSTUDY **keeps every row** and saves that full set.

If the model returns **more than 20% extra**, PSTUDY **reduces** the list to your targets by sampling **evenly** across the model’s order (so you are not limited to only the **first** chunk of items). The saved deck uses that reduced set. In that situation your browser may also download **two** PSTUDY **.txt** files: one with the **full** model output (`-ai-full`) and one with the **same subset** PSTUDY saved (`-ai-pstudy-capped`). You can re-import either file from **Import .txt** if you want the full set in PSTUDY or want to trim cards yourself. Some browsers limit multiple downloads; if one file is missing, check the download bar or site permissions.

**Always review** generated items in the deck editor before exams or sharing—AI output can contain mistakes or awkward wording.
help.bodyExams:
Under **Exams**, create a timed exam from one of your decks. You choose duration, exam type (multiple choice or straight answer), how questions are phrased, and invite people by **email**. Each invite gets a private link.

**My assigned exams** lists invites that match your logged-in email. Open an exam from there or from the link in your mail. Submitting ends the attempt; you can review as allowed by the exam settings.

Organisers manage invites and results on the exam detail page; examinees can **delete** an assignment from their list (the invite is revoked), which is equivalent to declining the invitation.
help.bodyCommunity:
**PstudyCommunity** lists decks that authors marked as shared. Search by title, field, or topic, and narrow by **Deck language**—select one or more languages you read so decks in German or French (for example) stay out of the list.

Authors set the deck’s language in the deck editor; decks with no language still appear if you leave all language boxes unchecked, or if you turn on “Include decks with no language set” while filtering.

**Copy to my decks** duplicates a deck into your account so you can edit and practice it privately.

Each listing shows **Draft** or **Checked**. These match the **PstudyCommunity quality** workflow: **Draft** until a review is completed; **Checked** after an invited reviewer has finished (see **PstudyCommunity quality and peer review** below).

On **My decks**, any deck you own that is **Shared** also shows the same **Draft** / **Checked** badge beside the item count.
help.bodyPeerReview:
Shared decks can carry a **quality** label for PstudyCommunity: **Draft** or **Checked**. This is independent of whether **you** keep a **private** copy after using **Copy to my decks**—it describes the **original shared listing** the author maintains.

**Where it appears.** **PstudyCommunity** cards show **Draft** or **Checked**. **My decks** shows the same badge for decks you marked **Shared**. In the **deck editor**, turn on **Share with PstudyCommunity** to publish; the header then shows **Draft** or **Checked** when relevant.

**Draft** means the deck is offered on PstudyCommunity but no invited peer has finished a structured corrections pass yet. **Checked** means an invited reviewer opened their link, edited item text and pictures within the allowed rules, saved, and **marked the review complete**; the author is notified by email and listings update.

**Inviting a reviewer.** In the deck editor, if the deck is **shared** and still **Draft**, use **Peer review** (toolbar). Enter the reviewer’s **email** and send—like exam invitations, they should log in with that email. Once a deck is **Checked**, this invite option is no longer shown from the editor.

**Reviewer role.** From the invite link, reviewers may fix typos and answer content and update images, but they **cannot add or remove items** (rows). They save changes, then complete the review so the deck becomes **Checked** for everyone.
help.bodyMyCommunities:
**MyCommunities** is for decks shared **inside your organization** (for example a school, company, or team). It is separate from **PstudyCommunity**, which lists decks shared with **everyone** using PSTUDY.

**Where to find it.** Use **MyCommunities** in the header to open your organization’s **library** of shared decks. If you are an **admin**, you also have **Manage MyCommunities** to add people and send invitations.

**Membership.** You only see MyCommunities content when your account belongs to an organization. An admin can **add someone who already has a PSTUDY account** (by email) or **send an email invitation**; the invitee should sign in with the **same email address** the invitation was sent to, then accept the link. If email is configured on the server, invitations may arrive by mail; otherwise the organiser may share the link another way.

**Sharing a deck.** In the **deck editor**, open **MyCommunities sharing** (shown when you belong to at least one organization). Pick the **organization**, choose **who can see** the deck (**all members** or **teachers only**), and save. This is independent of **Share with PstudyCommunity**—you can use one, the other, or both.

**Verification.** For **all members** visibility, **students** usually see a deck only after a **teacher** or **admin** **verifies** it (from the library or while viewing the deck). **Teachers only** keeps the listing off student views by design.

**Copying.** Like on PstudyCommunity, members can **copy** a shared deck into **My decks**; that copy is **private** unless you share it again.
help.bodySpeech:
Speech recognition works best in **Chrome** or **Edge**. Practice **Speak** and **Listen** may be limited on other browsers. If your host enables Google Cloud speech, deck authors can use options that favour recognition of answers in the deck.

Flashcards can use browser-only or Google speech to dictate your answer—see the flashcard speech control in practice when flashcard mode is on.
help.bodyMoreHelp:
Product and company details are available on the marketing site.

Home page: link from the logo or **Home** above. Account issues: use **Log in** and password reset on the sign-in page.
help.bodyContact: Send a message about PSTUDY Online, technical issues, or feedback. We normally reply by email.
help.contactNotConfigured: The contact form is not configured on this server yet.

deckReview.reviewStatusHint: Publication state (draft/checked) is separate from whether a peer review is in progress.
deckReview.resubmitSuccess: Reviewer notified. Your deck is marked as resubmitted.
deckReview.requestHint: Invite another user by email to correct this shared deck. They can edit items but not add or remove rows. When they finish, the deck is marked checked and you are notified by email. Same idea as exam invitations — they must log in with the email you enter.
deckReview.inviteCreatedNoEmail: Invitation created, but email could not be sent (configure RESEND_API_KEY). Share the review link manually from the server response if needed.
deckReview.bannerBody: Correct typos or answer text only. You cannot add or remove items. Save your changes, then mark the review complete so the author is notified and the deck becomes “checked” on PstudyCommunity.
deckReview.confirmComplete: Mark this review complete? The deck author will be notified and the deck will show as checked on PstudyCommunity.
deckReview.confirmVerify: Verify this deck? This marks it as verified on PstudyCommunity (teacher-reviewed).
deckReview.verifyHint: Teachers can mark a deck as verified (stronger than checked).
deckReview.unavailable: This review link is invalid or you need to log in with the invited email address.
deckReview.requestChangesBody: The deck stays a draft. The author can revise and use “Resubmit for review” when ready. They receive an email with your note (if you add one).
deckReview.feedbackOptionalPlaceholder: Optional: what should they fix or clarify?
deckReview.confirmRequestChanges: Send this feedback to the author now?
deckReview.requestChangesSuccess: Feedback sent. The author has been notified.
deckReview.completeDisabledUntilResubmit: The author must address your feedback and resubmit before you can mark the review complete.

banner.underConstruction: PSTUDY Online is under construction. Features may change.
